title: Hierarchy of Cardinality for Sets
description: A visual representation of how sets are categorized based on their cardinality, from finite sets to different types of infinite sets, illustrating the concept of countability and the hierarchy of infinities.
elements: Uncountably Infinite Sets (e.g., Cardinality of the Continuum c)
relationships: Finite Sets are a proper subset of Countable Sets
Countably Infinite Sets are a proper subset of Infinite Sets but have a smaller cardinality than Uncountably Infinite Sets
The cardinality of the set of real numbers (c) is greater than the cardinality of natural numbers (ℵ₀)
